name: New York City
summary: New York City is the biggest city in the United States, packed with towering skyscrapers, yellow taxis and famous landmarks. The giant green Statue of Liberty welcomes visitors from her harbour island.
funFacts: The Statue of Liberty was a giant gift from France in 1886 and turned green over time because she is made of copper.
The Empire State Building has 1,860 stairs and was the tallest building on Earth for 40 years until 1970.
Central Park is bigger than the whole country of Monaco and has 21 playgrounds, a zoo, lakes and rowboats inside it.
The New York City subway has 472 stations, more than any other subway in the world, and runs trains 24 hours every day.
Peregrine falcons, the fastest birds on Earth, nest on New York skyscrapers and dive at over 300 kilometres per hour to catch pigeons.
thingsToDo: Catch the ferry to Liberty Island to stand beneath the giant green Statue of Liberty.
Zoom up the Empire State Building in a super-fast lift to see Manhattan's whole skyline below.
Walk across the Brooklyn Bridge from Manhattan, then grab a New York pizza slice on the other side.
Spend a morning in Central Park, rowing boats, riding the carousel and spotting cheeky grey squirrels.
Wander through Times Square at night to see thousands of giant glowing billboards flashing all around you.
Visit the 9/11 Memorial pools beside One World Trade Center, the tallest building in the United States.

wildlife.name: Eastern Grey Squirrel
wildlife.description: Bushy-tailed grey rodent that scampers up trees in Central Park hunting for acorns.

wildlife.name: Raccoon
wildlife.description: Furry night-time visitor with a black bandit mask and clever paws that open bins.

wildlife.name: Mandarin Duck
wildlife.description: Brightly coloured duck from Asia that famously appeared in Central Park in 2018.

wildlife.name: Peregrine Falcon
wildlife.description: Speedy hunting bird that nests high on New York skyscrapers and bridges.

localWord.meaning: A New York way of saying 'forget about it' — used when something is amazing or just not worth worrying about.

images.alt: The Statue of Liberty stands tall on her island holding her torch up to the sky.
The Empire State Building rises high above the rooftops of New York City.
Times Square glows at night with giant colourful billboards and bright neon lights.
Belvedere Castle sits on a rocky hill in leafy Central Park.
The Brooklyn Bridge stretches across the water with its big stone towers and crisscrossing cables.
One World Trade Center, the tallest skyscraper in the United States, towers into the sky.
An Eastern grey squirrel sits in a New York park nibbling on a snack.
A raccoon with its black bandit mask peers out from the undergrowth.
A brightly coloured Mandarin duck swims on a pond in Central Park.
A peregrine falcon perched on a branch, ready to take off.
